name: dataclasses-json (Dataclass JSON Marshaling)

performance.notes: Slower than mashumaro or msgspec; uses marshmallow under the hood

agent_readiness.notes: Simple JSON (de)serialization for Python dataclasses. from dataclasses import dataclass. from dataclasses_json import dataclass_json. @dataclass_json. @dataclass. class User: name: str; age: int. user = User.from_json('{"name": "Alice", "age": 30}'). user.to_json(). User.from_dict({'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30}). user.to_dict(). schema = User.schema(). schema.dump(user). schema.load(dict). Optional fields: from typing import Optional. Nested: field types auto-resolved. Custom: config(field_name='alt_name', encoder=..., decoder=...). List: User.schema().load(list_of_dicts, many=True).

gotchas: Slower than mashumaro/msgspec — uses marshmallow as backend for schema
Optional field handling changed across versions — check docs for version
Nested dataclasses must also be decorated with @dataclass_json
No validation — from_json() with wrong types may silently coerce or error
Limited active development — mashumaro or cattrs preferred for new projects
best_when: Simple dataclass JSON serialization without pydantic dependency
Quick prototyping where from_json() / to_json() convenience is valued
When dataclasses are used but pydantic is too heavy
avoid_when: New projects — mashumaro is better maintained and faster
summary: dataclasses-json adds from_json()/to_json()/from_dict()/to_dict() to Python dataclasses. @dataclass_json @dataclass. Marshmallow backend. schema() for bulk (de)serialization. MIT by lidatong. Simple JSON marshaling for dataclasses. Slower than mashumaro. Limited validation. Nested classes need decoration too. For new projects prefer mashumaro or cattrs for better performance.
